reasoning: The effective yield of the ICE BofA AA US Corporate Index behaves largely as a random walk over short-to-medium time horizons. Without real-time data for late October 2025, the most rigorous base rate for an asset price / yield movement is a 50% probability for either an increase or decrease. However, incorporating the long-term macroeconomic consensus that the US Federal Reserve may be going through an easing/rate-cutting cycle in late 2024 and 2025, there is a slight structural headwind for yields, nudging the probability of an increase slightly below 50%. Following the strict instructions that uncertainty must increase with forecast horizon and distant dates must be closer to 0.5, the short-term probability is modeled at 0.48 with mean reversion mapping the longest-term horizon exactly to 0.50.
